title_en: Why Britons now trust weather forecasters
title_zh: 英国人为何开始信任天气预报员
rubric_en: Credit greater accuracy and better communication
rubric_zh: 更高的准确度与更好的沟通

paragraphs.en: Chatting about the weather is a national pastime in Britain—and successive heatwaves have recently given Britons lots to discuss. In the past, the obsession did not translate into faith in weather forecasters. Memories lingered of the “great storm” of 1987, a deadly cyclone. The BBC’s best-known forecaster, Michael Fish (pictured), said on air that a woman had called in worrying there might be a “hurricane” approaching Britain. “If you’re watching, don’t worry, there isn’t!” he quipped. Hours later the storm struck.
paragraphs.zh: 在英国，聊天气是全国性的日常爱好，而最近一连串的热浪也让英国人有了足够的话题。过去，这种执着并没有转化为对天气预报员的信任。人们仍记得1987年的“超级风暴”，那场致命的气旋。BBC 最著名的预报员迈克尔·费舍（Michael Fish，图中）当时在节目中说，一位女士打电话进来，担心有“飓风”要来临英国。“如果你在看，不用担心，不会有！”他开玩笑道。几个小时后风暴就袭击了英国。

paragraphs.en: YouGov, a pollster, found in 2017 that barely half of people trusted forecasters to give them accurate information about the weather. But that has changed. A recent YouGov survey finds that now 62% of Britons trust them and just 25% are sceptical—still far behind trust in nurses (86%), but well ahead of economists (37%), let alone politicians (a mere 4%). The change is partly because forecasts are more accurate than ever thanks to advances in technology, suggests Alex Deakin of the Met Office. It also helps that fewer people today remember the 1987 storm.
paragraphs.zh: 民调机构 YouGov 在2017年发现，只有略少于一半的人相信天气预报员能给出准确的信息。但这种情况已经改变。YouGov 最近的一项调查显示，如今62%的英国人信任他们，只有25%的人持怀疑态度——这仍远低于对护士的信任度（86%），但远高于对经济学家的信任（37%），更不用说对政治人物的信任仅为4%了。Met Office 的亚历克斯·迪金（Alex Deakin）表示，变化部分源于技术进步带来的“更高准确率”。同样重要的是，如今更少人还记得1987年的风暴。

paragraphs.en: Indeed, that storm prompted investment in weather-forecasting tech and communications protocols. Liam Dutton, a weather presenter for Channel 4 News, says the model he relies on divides Britain into squares of 2km (1.2 miles) a side, whereas a decade ago the highest resolution possible was 12km. The average accuracy of a forecast four days into the future is comparable to that of a one-day forecast in the 1990s. Under-25s, who grew up in an era of reliable forecasts, are by a large margin most likely to trust forecasters.
paragraphs.zh: 事实上，那场风暴促成了对天气预报技术和通信流程的投资。Channel 4 News 的天气主播利亚姆·达顿（Liam Dutton）说，他所依赖的模型把英国划分为边长为2公里（1.2英里）的网格，而十年前可用的最高分辨率是12公里。如今四天后的天气预报平均准确度，相当于20世纪90年代的一天预报准确度。那些在可靠预报时代成长起来的25岁以下人群，最有可能信任天气预报员，他们的信任比例远高于其他年龄层。

paragraphs.en: Climate change has raised the stakes by causing more extreme, sometimes life-threatening shocks such as prolonged heatwaves and sudden heavy downpours. “Being more accurate when it comes to those extreme events builds trust,” says Mr Deakin (though climate change also makes forecasting trickier). Since 2015, following the practice in America since the 1950s, the Met Office has given names to the biggest storms. The most recent was Storm Dave, in April. The agency believes this has helped engage the public. There are also colour-coded warnings for heat. A red alert in June was Britain’s second, after one in 2022.
paragraphs.zh: 气候变化使事态更复杂，也让极端天气事件更常见，这些事件有时会威胁生命，比如长期热浪和突发的大雨。“在这些极端事件中预测得更准确，就能建立信任，”迪金先生说（尽管气候变化也让预报变得更难）。自2015年起，英国气象局沿袭美国上世纪50年代以来的做法，开始为最大的风暴命名。最近一次的是4月的戴夫风暴（Storm Dave）。该机构认为这有助于吸引公众关注。该机构还推出了颜色分级的高温警报。6月发布的红色警报是英国第二次红色警报，此前上一次发生在2022年。

paragraphs.en: Though it is now easier to predict the British weather, dealing with it remains a challenge. Older houses are built to hoard heat in the chilly winter; regulations mean that even most new-build homes lack air conditioning. The government has at least started to offer grants for homeowners to install heat pumps that also have a cooling function, which had previously been excluded from the most popular subsidy scheme. Nonetheless, when the mercury passes 30°C most Britons will continue to lose their cool. ■
paragraphs.zh: 虽然如今更容易预测英国天气，但应对天气仍然很有挑战。老房子建造时以在寒冷的冬季储存热量为目标；监管规定也意味着即使是大多数新建住宅，也缺乏空调。政府至少已经开始为房主提供补贴，让其安装兼具降温功能的热泵，而这类设备此前未被纳入最受欢迎的补贴方案。尽管如此，当温度超过30℃时，大多数英国人仍会继续热得难以忍受。■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in Britain, sign up to Blighty, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需获取关于英国最重要新闻的更多专家解析，请订阅《Blighty》，这是我们的每周付费会员通讯。
